message:
fix(debug): clear transient state when debug overlays are disabled

All of the debug/overlay surfaces had the same latent bug: disabling
the debugger (or toggling it off, or rendering into an empty area)
left behind stale records/hover/dirty bookkeeping from the previous
session.  The next time the user enabled it they'd see the old
residue mixed with fresh data — most visibly on the debug overlay,
where stale highlight rectangles would flash before being overwritten.

Changes:

crates/ftui-layout/src/debug.rs (LayoutDebugger):
- set_enabled(): switch from store() to swap() so we can observe the
  previous state and call clear() on the falling edge.
- toggle(): route through set_enabled() instead of a bare fetch_xor
  so it gets the same clear-on-disable behavior for free.
- record() / record_grid(): re-check the enabled flag *after* taking
  the mutex.  Without this, a concurrent disable-then-clear could
  race with a record call and reintroduce stale entries.
- Tests: add assertions that disable wipes both record streams, plus
  a dedicated debugger_disable_clears_grid_records case.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/debug_overlay.rs (DebugOverlayState):
- set_enabled(): on the disable edge, also clear() entries and
  set_hover(None) so the next enable starts from a clean slate.
- toggle(): route through set_enabled() so the tracing log and the
  clear path stay consistent.
- record(): move the enabled check inside the mutex guard for the
  same race-window reason as LayoutDebugger::record.
- update_hover_from_event(): drop the
  `matches!(mouse.kind, Moved | Drag)` filter so hover is updated
  on *any* mouse event (clicks, scrolls, drags).  The previous
  filter meant clicking a widget while the overlay was active would
  leave hover frozen at the last drag position, which looked broken.
- Tests: new disabling_overlay_clears_transient_state,
  set_hover_while_disabled_tracks_position, and
  update_hover_from_click_event_sets_position; also flip
  overlay_disabled_does_not_clear_entries →
  overlay_disabled_keeps_state_cleared and
  update_hover_from_scroll_event_does_not_change_position →
  update_hover_from_scroll_event_updates_position to match the
  new contract.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/layout_debugger.rs (&mut-variant):
- set_enabled(): mirror the atomic variant — observe the previous
  value and clear() on the falling edge.
- Tests: extend the existing enable/disable test and add
  debugger_disable_clears_stale_records.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/help.rs (Help StatefulWidget):
- render(): when called with an empty area, clear not just `cache`
  but also dirty_rects / dirty_indices / enabled_indices.  The old
  code cleared only the cache, so if a parent layout briefly
  collapsed us to 0×0 we'd re-render with dirty state that pointed
  at rects outside the new area.
- Tests: extend the existing empty-area test to verify all four
  pieces of transient state get wiped.

No behavior change in the steady-state "enabled" path of any
component; the fix is strictly about the enabled→disabled transition
and the empty-area rendering case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
